steps.title: Find the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content: The vertex of a parabola lies halfway between its roots: $$x=\frac{-5+(-1)}{2}=-3$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The value $-3$ lies in the interval $-5\lt x\lt1$. B is correct.

steps.title: Eliminate endpoint traps
steps.content: Choices C and D start with $-3 \lt x$, which does not include $x=-3$ because the inequality is strict. Choice A (‘$-6 \lt x \lt -5$’) is left of both roots. Only the credited option actually contains $-3$.
